calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Fifth Account And Report For The Period January 1, 2019 Through December 31, 2019; Petition To Terminate Trust; And For Final Dis
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Charlotte Ito appearing by Zoom for Sylvia Donovan (Successor Trustee). Alan Sparer (Co-Counsel) appearing by Zoom for Sylvia Donovan (Successor Trustee).

The Court stated that before it today is the Petition to Approve Fifth Account and Report for the Period January 1, 2019 to December 31, 2019; Petition to Terminate Trust; And for Final Distribution. The Court addressed Ms. Ito stating that it had received the additional filing, that it is satisfied with venue, that the mosted changed circumstances had arisen from the settlement, that it is guided by the trustor's intent, that there is way more than $10,000.00 left, and that the change of circumstance doesn't justify a termination of the trust. Ms. Ito stated that she did not think that the settlor thought this through, that two condos went to Darryl Liu, that the remaining condo was sold, that it was removed from the City's Below Market Rate (BMR) program, that they are left with the St. Paul Apartments, that there is no onsite manager, and that it is more or less like an annuity. Mr. Sparer stated that the settlement is voluntary, that it is after years and years of litigation, that settlement is not always voluntary, and that this was not a deliberate move regarding the trust. The Court stated that it does not know a lot about the settlement, that it is coming in at the middle for which it does not have an accounting, that the settlor wanted was it to be in place until $10,000.00 is left, and that the guidepost here is the settlor's intent. The Court then DENIED the Petition to Approve Fifth Account and Report for the Period January 1, 2019 to December 31, 2019; Petition to Terminate Trust; And for Final Distribution stating that it gave this careful consideration.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition To Approve Fifth Account And Report For The Period January 1, 2019 Through December 31, 2019; Petition To Terminate Trust; And For Final Dis
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Continued To Aug-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charlotte Ito appeared by telephone for Sylvia Donovan (Trustee).
Alan Sparer appeared by telephone for Sylvia Donovan (Trustee).

The Court stated that it does not see how it can order distribution of this trust, that trusts are supposed to be administered outside of the Court, that this trust has been, that it now has this accounting petition, that it does not have a starting point and that it has no idea what the settlement is. Ms. Ito stated that a declaration was filed on July 17th and that papers were dropped in the box yesterday, that it seems like the declaration hasn't been reviewed, that purpose of the petition and the accounting is that it would be in the best interests of the Trust and Trust beneficiaries to distribute the assets and terminate the Trust, that the trust provisions provide that the trust continue to be administered and can only be terminated if the balance is $10,000.00 or less, that distribution is not being requested pursuant to the settlement agreement and that some of the beneficiaries are no longer beneficiaries of the trust. Ms. Ito also stated that there was litigation involving this trust in a different case number, that four accountings were never adjudicated because that was part of the settlement agreement and that it was filed in a different case number because they were not the same parties. In addition, Ms. Ito stated that the $10,000.00 amount does not take into consideration the costs to administer this trust, that two properties were distributed, that one condo was sold, and that the only asset of the trust is a 1/6 interest in a partnership that owns an apartment building that is already being managed, a bank account and a security. Furthermore, Ms. Ito stated that there are changed circumstances, that the Settlor had no idea that the properties would leave the trust and that there would be intensive litigation between the beneficiaries and the Trustee, and that the petitions are because the Trustee is going to do something in controversy of the trust. Ms. Ito also stated that there will need to be separation into three trust and it will create additional costs, and addressed the issue of venue stating that when the trust administration began all of the assets were in Californina and the principal place of administration was in Californinia, and confirmed that the Trustee is in Oregon. The Petition to Approve Fifth Account and Report, etc. is continued to August 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the declaration.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
